kicker: PT LEVEL I // LIGHTING & LIGHT METERS

beats.narration: Fluorescent penetrant works in the dark for a reason. A speck of dye glows under ultraviolet light, fighting every stray lumen in the booth.

payload.title: LIGHTING IS DATA
payload.sub: In fluorescent PT, light is a measured process variable.

beats.narration: So two numbers govern Type One work. UV-A at the surface, at least a thousand microwatts per square centimeter. Ambient white light, two footcandles or less.
Prove it with calibrated meters. The sensor sits at the surface, facing the lamp, at the working distance. Intensity falls roughly with the square.
Know your source. Mercury vapor needs warm-up to steady. LED runs cooler but must be certified. A cracked filter leaks hazardous UV, retire it.
So treat every reading as data. Measure at the surface, write it down, and if it is below minimum, stop and fix the light.

payload.sub: You never inspect your way past a failed light check.
